role: Political activist; co-leader of Nation of Iran Party; assassinated 1998
bio: Parvaneh Eskandari Forouhar was a political activist and co-leader of the [Nation of Iran Party](/orgs/nation-of-iran-party) alongside her husband [Dariush Forouhar](/people/dariush-forouhar). Active in Iranian politics since the 1950s, she was a fierce advocate for women's rights and democratic governance. On the night of 22 November 1998, [Ministry of Intelligence](/orgs/ministry-of-intelligence) agents entered the Forouhar home and murdered both husband and wife — she was stabbed 25 times. Their double assassination was one of the most shocking acts of the [chain murders](/events/chain-murders-iran-1998) and galvanised the reformist press and civil society into demanding accountability from the intelligence services.
editorial_notes: Born 1938. Wife of Dariush Forouhar. Assassinated 22 November 1998 — stabbed 25 times by MOIS agents.
